home.iosInstructions:
1. Open the website in the Safari app.
2. Tap the "Share" button (the square with an upward arrow at the bottom of the screen).
3. Scroll through the options and select "Add to Home Screen".
4. Confirm by tapping "Add" in the top-right corner.

test.title: Network quality tests
test.section: Would you like to know the quality of the network in a specific location or plan your next train journey?
test.note: Arcep regularly tests the quality level of the network at specific locations and on numerous transport routes.
test.infoTest: During the 2022 campaign, 490 000 tests were carried out in mainland France.
test.webBrowsingDescription: A web navigation test consists of measuring the loading time of several web pages, such as Wikipedia, Météo France, Le Monde, etc.
test.webBrowsingDescriptionMore: All these pages make up a panel. A panel is made up of all these pages. A threshold of 10 seconds is set. Pages that load in less than 10 seconds are considered successful, and those that take longer than 10 seconds fail. The test is considered successful if more than 75% of the pages in the panel are successful. It is considered partially successful when between 30% and 75% of the pages in the panel are successful.
test.streamingDescription: A streaming test measures video playback quality by evaluating resolution, smoothness and buffering time.
test.downloadDescription: A download speed test evaluates the speed at which data is downloaded from the Internet to a device, measured in megabits per second (Mbps).
test.uploadDescription: An upload test involves transmitting a file from a phone to a remote server. This is how you might send a selfie to a friend, for example. This test checks that a 1 MB file is transmitted completely to the server in less than 30 seconds, without any alteration to its content.
test.testDownload: Part of download speed tests greater than 8 Mbit/s
test.pageLoad: Page loads in less than 10 seconds
test.success-web-description: The 10 web pages loaded there took on average less than 5 seconds to fully load.
test.partialsuccess-web-description: The 10 web pages loaded there took on average less than 10 seconds to fully load.
test.fail-web-description: The 10 web pages loaded there took on average more than 10 seconds to fully load.
test.success-voix-description: The call was held for 120 seconds and the lowest MOS score is greater than 2.1. A MOS score is a rating given by an algorithm that evaluates the fidelity of an input signal compared to an output signal. It ranges from 0 to 5. The input signal is divided into 10-second subsamples. For the test to pass, all subsamples must have a MOS score greater than 2.1.
test.partialsuccess-voix-description: The call was maintained for 2 min, but the lowest MOS score was less than 2.1.
test.fail-voix-description: The call was not maintained for 2 min.
test.stat-web-subtitle: Loading a page in less than 5 seconds
test.stat-voix-subtitle: Communication maintained for 2 minutes without audible disturbance
test.legendeTitle: Arcep measurements taken with 2G/3G/4G/5G terminals from
test.testrealised: tests have been successfully carried out in this area
test.testrealisedsuccess: tests have been successfully carried out in this area
test.testrealisedpartialsuccess: tests were carried out with partial success in this area
test.test-debit-inf-3: tests have a download rate of less than 3 Mbit/s
test.test-debit-in-3-8: tests have a download rate of between 3 and 8 Mbit/s
test.test-debit-in-8-30: tests have a download rate of between 8 and 30 Mbit/s
test.test-debit-sup-30: tests have a download rate greater than 30 Mbit/s
test.click-on-point: Click on a point to find out more
test.majorite-label-dl: In the majority, the tests carried out here measured flow rates
test.majorite-label-other: In the majority, the tests carried out here are
test.test-date: Measurements carried out by Arcep from
test.resume-legend-help-description: The color of the dot representing the test result on the selected location corresponds to the sum of the flow classes allowing the majority (50%) to be exceeded. In the event of a tie, the weaker class is chosen.
test.transport-info: How good is the network in the transport networks you regularly use?
test.transport-more-info: These maps allow you to select transport routes and compare the performance of different operators networks, both for web browsing and for phone calls. These measures are the result of rigorous tests carried out in real conditions by technicians mandated by Arcep. Quality of service in transport, whether by car or train, can vary according to vehicle speed, the tightness of the walls or the number of users wishing to access the network at the same time.

antenne.title: Antennas and deployments
antenne.section: Would you like to consult the location and characteristics of 3G, 4G or 5G network antennas in the area?
antenne.info: The map displays supports, such as pylons, water towers, roofs...
antenne.moreInfo: A medium can host several sites, belonging to different operators. The sites are made up of antennas (3G, 4G, 5G), which allow you to receive the network on your mobile. Click on the symbols on the map for more information.
antenne.nbrSitebyoprateur: Total number of sites per operator and best available technology
antenne.infoFrance: France has [nbSites] sites. These are sets of antennas located in the same place and making it possible to bring the network to an area.
antenne.moreInfoMap: To display more informations, select a support on the map.
antenne.notAvailableTransport: You have selected a transport axis in the search bar. Theoretical coverage data for transport axes is not available.

antenne.legend-stat: Sites are counted only once for the best technology they have: if a site has 5G technology in 3.5 GHz and 4G, it is only counted in the "5G in 3.5 GHz" category.

zone.title: Areas to be covered
zone.section: Would you like to find out more about the areas and points of interest to be covered by operators?
zone.zac-description: Arcep implements and monitors the obligations set for each mobile operator
zone.zac-moredescription: These obligations are included in frequency use authorizations which allow operators to operate their networks. Among these obligations is the coverage of certain areas and road and rail axes. In this menu, you can view the areas that are subject to coverage obligations and monitor the progress of the deployment of operators.
zone.zac-point-interet-dcc: Points of interest of the targeted coverage system (DCC)
zone.zac-description-poi-dcc: The Targeted Coverage System (DCC) is part of the obligations of the Mobile New Deal. Its objective is to ensure coverage of 5,000 areas by each of the four operators: Bouygues Telecom, Free Mobile, Orange and SFR. Each year, the minister responsible for electronic communications signs one or more lists of areas to be covered on the advice of local project teams working for local authorities.
zone.zac-nombre-site-demande: Number of sites requested by the government to be put into service
zone.zac-site-mis-service: Sites put into service as part of the Targeted Coverage System
zone.zac-description-label: Adding My Mobile Network to the home screen allows you to download the application in the form of a progressive web app (PWA) on your mobile (see part 5.6 in the CCTP). Finally, the footer is an element common to all pages.
zone.zac-number-site-gov: Number of sites requested by the government per operator
zone.description-first: In January 2018, Arcep and the Government announced commitments made by operators to accelerate mobile coverage of territories: this is the Mobile New Deal.
zone.description-second: Among the measures of the New Mobile Deal, the targeted coverage system allows local authorities to define areas to be covered, materialized by points of interest, in which operators are required to provide very high-speed mobile access services. (4G), no later than 24 months after the date of publication of the order by the Minister. In this menu you can view information about the area you have selected to cover.
zone.site-ask: Sites requested to cover the area :
zone.site-service: Sites already put into service in the area :
zone.site-not-appear: The sites to be built do not yet appear on the map. Operators have 24 months to deploy them.

signalement.title: I alert Arcep
signalement.section: Sometimes things just do not work as planned. This function allows you to report a network anomaly and view on the map the areas where other reports have been made.
signalement.reportDescription: Are you experiencing a coverage problem, network quality degradation, or breakdown? Do you notice a difference between the information published and your experience?
signalement.help: Position the alert point on the map.
signalement.descritpionResume: The reports come from requests from users received by Arcep, in particular on its "I alert Arcep" platform.
signalement.descritpionMore: They indicate mobile network problems (coverage, breakdown, degradation of quality of service). Their display on My mobile network is updated every month and covers the period of the last six months before publication.
signalement.statisticsDescription: Reports of mobile network problems in
signalement.infoPageResume: Number of reports recorded in this area over the last 6 months
signalement.click-on-hexagon: Click on a hexagon to find out more

qos.typetestdescvoix: A call test assesses the quality of voice communication on the basis of a Mean Opinion Score (MOS).
qos.typetestdescsms: An SMS test evaluates the time taken to receive an SMS from a device A to a device B.

couverture.title: What is theoretical coverage ?
couverture.description:
An operator's theoretical coverage is the result of digital simulation. 
The geography of the French territory (altimetry, vegetation cover, volume of buildings) is reproduced within a digital terrain model and then cut into squares of equal size. 
The position of radio antennas, the frequencies used and other parameters enable complex algorithms to predict which tiles will be covered and which will not. 
Each tile is assigned a theoretical level of service: very good coverage, good coverage, limited coverage or not covered. 
The maps are simulated by the operators and verified by Arcep. 
In the event of errors or incorrect settings, the maps are corrected. 
These maps remain the result of a simulation and are by nature imperfect. 
You can help us improve them by reporting a problem in the « Report » tab.

test.title: What is a network quality test ?
test.description:
To assess the quality of mobile networks, Arcep carries out extensive measurement campaigns in the local area every year. 
There are a wide variety of tests available to assess the performance of a mobile network: testing calls, web page loading, online video playback, and so on.
The dots displayed on the map represent the locations where these tests have been carried out. You can filter tests by operator and type of test.
The results of these tests are aggregated at macroscopic scales (Metropolitan France and regions) to enable you to compare the performance of mobile operators in the places where you live or travel.
You can find out more about how these tests are carried out on the dedicated page: « Quality tests ».

support.title: What is a site and what is a support ?
support.description:
A support is the structure on which operator sites are positioned. It can be a pylon, a water tower or a roof terrace, for example. 
A site is a group of antennas (usually three) positioned to enable an operator's customers to connect to its network. 
The same support can host the sites of several mobile operators. These are known as « co-located » sites . 
Supports are displayed on the map. If you click on a support, you will be able to consult the list of sites hosted on it. By clicking on a site, you can consult additional information such as the technologies activated on the site (2G, 3G, 4G, 5G) or the frequency bands currently in service.

zone.title: What is an area to be covered ?
zone.description:
Arcep imposes and monitors coverage in certain areas of the country. 
These may be roads or railroads whose coverage is deemed necessary, or areas of the country with little or no coverage to date. 
The points to be covered are chosen by ¨local project teams¨ working on behalf of local authorities, who are allocated a quota of points each year to target the least-covered areas of their territory. 
Once a point has been declared, operators have 24 months to deploy a site that will bring coverage to that location. 
You can follow the progress of operators' rollouts using the information in the dedicated menu.

signalement.title: What is an Arcep report ?
signalement.description:
Are you experiencing difficulties with your operator's network? You can report them to us via this menu.
 Mobile networks can experience variations in performance depending on the number of people wishing to connect simultaneously, and can even break down. 
Reports enable us to refine our diagnostics and identify areas frequently subject to outages or performance degradations. 
Areas that have recently been reported are displayed on the map. 
Your personal data will not be communicated.
